file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for HPID (HP ID) authentication operations including sign-in, sign-up, account verification, and country selection dismissal. Provides static utility methods for automating HPID authentication flows through the UI using Playwright page interactions with the HPID page object.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for HPID authentication operations including dismissing country selector popups, performing sign-in with email and password, completing full sign-in flows, signing up new users with account details, confirming account verification codes, and completing full sign-up flows. All methods are static and work with Playwright page instances and HPIDPage page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: HPIDHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page where the country selector popup may appear

functions.what_it_does: Attempts to dismiss the country selector popup that may appear on the HPID page. Creates an HPIDPage instance with the provided page. Then tries to click the first country selector button using hpid_page.country_selector_button.first.click(). If the button is not found or any exception occurs during the click, the exception is caught and silently passed, allowing the flow to continue. This is a defensive method used to handle optional popups that may or may not appear.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HPID authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HPID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the core HPID sign-in flow by entering credentials and submitting the form. Creates an HPIDPage instance. First fills the username field with the provided email using hpid_page.username.fill(email). Then clicks the username form submit button using hpid_page.user_name_form_submit.click() to proceed to the password screen. Next fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Finally clicks the sign-in button using hpid_page.sign_in.click() to complete the authentication. This is the core sign-in logic without any pre-steps like country dismissal.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: User email address for HPID authentication

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: User password for HPID authentication

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HPID sign-in flow including all pre-steps and the actual sign-in. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup that may appear. Then creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the sign-in option button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.sign_in_option(timeout=20000) and clicks it to navigate to the sign-in form. Finally calls HPIDHelper.sign_in(page, email, password) to perform the actual credential entry and form submission. This is the complete sign-in flow used in test scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address for the new HPID account

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new HPID account

functions.what_it_does: Performs the HPID sign-up flow by filling the registration form with user details. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Fills the first name field with the hardcoded value 'InstantInk' using hpid_page.firstName.fill('InstantInk'). Fills the last name field with the hardcoded value 'User' using hpid_page.lastName.fill('User'). Fills the email field with the provided email using hpid_page.email.fill(email). Fills the password field with the provided password using hpid_page.password.fill(password). Clicks the market checkbox using hpid_page.market.click() to accept terms. Finally clicks the create account button using hpid_page.create_account_button.click() to submit the registration form. This creates a new HPID account but does not handle verification code confirmation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address where the verification code was sent

functions.what_it_does: Handles the HPID account verification code confirmation process with retry logic. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the submit code button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.submit_code(timeout=20000). Sets up retry logic with 3 attempts and 10-second wait time between attempts. For each attempt, waits for the specified wait time using page.wait_for_timeout(wait_time * 1000). Fetches the verification code from the email using common.fetch_verification_code(email). If a verification code is retrieved, fills the code field using hpid_page.code.fill(verification_code) and clicks the submit code button using hpid_page.submit_code.click(). Logs a success message with the email, stack, country code, and language code from GlobalState. Breaks out of the retry loop on success. If an exception occurs, logs a warning with the attempt number and continues to the next retry. If all retries fail and no verification code was obtained, logs an error message and returns. Finally waits for the page to fully load using page.wait_for_load_state('load').

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: tenant_email
parameters.description: Email address for the new tenant account to be created

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete end-to-end HPID sign-up flow including all steps from country dismissal to account verification. First calls HPIDHelper.dismiss_select_country(page) to dismiss any country selector popup. Creates an HPIDPage instance. Waits for the create account button to be visible with a 20-second timeout using hpid_page.wait.create_account_button_data(timeout=20000) and clicks it to navigate to the sign-up form. Calls HPIDHelper.sign_up(page, tenant_email, common.DEFAULT_PASSWORD) to fill and submit the registration form using the provided tenant email and the default password from the common module. Finally calls HPIDHelper.confirm_account_code(page, tenant_email) to handle the verification code confirmation process. This is the complete sign-up flow used in test scenarios to create and verify new HPID accounts.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a well-structured helper module following the static utility pattern. All methods are static and do not require class instantiation. The module provides both granular methods (sign_in, sign_up) and composite methods (complete_sign_in, complete_sign_up) that combine multiple steps. The confirm_account_code method includes robust retry logic for handling email verification codes. The dismiss_select_country method uses defensive exception handling to gracefully handle optional UI elements. This helper is designed to be used in test setup and authentication flows where HPID login or account creation is required.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for landing page interactions including navigation, privacy banner handling, section validation, OOBE and MOBE flow testing, hyperlink validation, cookie settings verification, FAQ validation, country selection, and emergency banner validation. Provides extensive static utility methods for automating landing page test scenarios across desktop and mobile viewports.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for landing page test automation including opening and accessing the landing page with privacy banner handling, verifying RAF (Refer-a-Friend) banners and footnotes, validating section visibility in viewport, testing consumer landing page OOBE and MOBE flows with navigation and section scrolling, validating hyperlinks in footer, testing cookie settings functionality, validating FAQ links and content, selecting countries, and validating emergency banners with different color schemes and icons for various countries. All methods are static and designed for use in test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: LandingPageHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like LandingPageHelper.access(page)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the Instant Ink landing page URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() with the URL from common._instantink_url and a 120-second timeout. After navigation, waits for the page load state to be 'load' using page.wait_for_load_state('load'). This is the basic navigation method without any additional handling of popups or banners.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the landing page and handles common popups and banners that may appear. First calls LandingPageHelper.open(page) to navigate to the landing page URL. Creates a PrivacyBannerPage instance and calls privacy_banner_page.accept_privacy_banner() to accept the privacy banner if it appears. Creates a LandingPage instance. Then attempts to close any promotional banner by clicking landing_page.close_banner.click(). If the close banner button is not found or cannot be clicked, catches the exception and prints an error message but continues execution. This is the recommended method for accessing the landing page in tests as it handles common UI elements.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Expected number of free months to be displayed in the RAF banner. Defaults to 1.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RAF) banner is visible on the landing page and contains the expected text about free months. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RAF banner element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_banner). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the banner is visible with a 30-second timeout. Extracts the banner text content using raf_banner.text_content(). Constructs the expected text string as 'You have {expected_months} free month of HP Instant Ink'. Asserts that the expected text is present in the banner text, raising an assertion error with details if not found. Logs a success message with the expected text using framework_logger.info(). Returns True on successful verification.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the Refer-a-Friend (RAF) footnote is visible on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the RAF footnote element using page.locator(landing_page.elements.raf_footnote). Uses Playwright's expect() to verify the footnote is visible with a 30-second timeout. This is a simple visibility check without text content validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: section_selector
parameters.description: CSS selector string for the section element to check

functions.what_it_does: Verifies whether a page section is currently visible within the browser viewport. Uses page.evaluate() to execute JavaScript in the browser context. The JavaScript code takes the section_selector as a parameter, uses document.querySelector() to find the element, returns false if the element is not found, gets the element's bounding rectangle using getBoundingClientRect(), and checks if the element is within the viewport by verifying that rect.top is greater than or equal to 0 and rect.bottom is less than or equal to the window inner height or document client height. Returns the boolean result indicating whether the section is in the viewport. This is used to verify that clicking navigation links properly scrolls sections into view.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions at specific steps. Called with step name, page, and screenshot_only=True.

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete consumer landing page OOBE (Out-of-Box Experience) flow for desktop viewport. Navigates to the landing page URL, accepts privacy banner if present, tests the Sign Up Now button functionality by clicking it and verifying HPID page appears, returns to landing page, then systematically tests each navigation tab (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs) by clicking each tab, waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the corresponding section is visible, checking if the section is in viewport, logging results, and optionally calling the callback function for screenshots. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors. This is a comprehensive OOBE flow test covering all major landing page sections and navigation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions at specific steps. Called with step name, page, and screenshot_only=True.

functions.what_it_does: Tests the complete consumer landing page MOBE (Mobile Experience) flow for mobile viewport. Similar to consumer_landing_page_oobe but uses mobile-specific locators. Navigates to the landing page URL, accepts privacy banner if present, clicks the mobile menu button, tests the mobile Sign Up button functionality by clicking it and verifying HPID page appears, returns to landing page, then systematically tests each mobile navigation tab by opening the mobile menu, clicking the tab (How It Works, Instant Paper Plan, Plans, FAQs), waiting 5 seconds for scroll animation, verifying the corresponding section is visible, checking if the section is in viewport, logging results, and optionally calling the callback function for screenshots. Uses mobile-specific locators like mobe_menu_button, mobe_sign_up_button, mobe_how_it_works_tab, etc. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors. This is a comprehensive MOBE flow test covering all major landing page sections and mobile navigation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page for desktop viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the never_worry_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Logs a success message. Optionally calls the callback function with 'never_worry', page, and screenshot_only=True. Wraps operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots or performing actions

functions.what_it_does: Validates that the 'Never worry about running out of ink again' section is visible on the landing page for mobile viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the mobe_never_work_section element to be visible with a 50-second timeout. Scrolls the section into view if needed using scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Logs a success message. Optionally calls the callback function with 'never_worry', page, and screenshot_only=True. Wraps operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors. Uses mobile-specific locator mobe_never_work_section.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots of opened pages

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for desktop viewport by clicking each link, verifying the opened page URL, optionally taking screenshots, and closing the new tab. Creates a LandingPage instance. Tests the following links in sequence: HP.com link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/home.html'), Privacy Statement link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/privacy.html'), HP Connected Terms of Use link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hpsmart.com/us/en/tou'), Cookie Settings link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/use-of-cookies.html'), Your Privacy Choices link (verifies URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/your-privacy-choices.html'), and Instant Ink Terms of Service link (verifies URL contains 'https://instantink-stage1.hpconnectedstage.com/us/en/terms'). For each link, waits for the element to be visible, scrolls it into view if needed, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab, clicks the link, waits for the new page to load, uses expect().to_have_url() with regex to verify the URL, logs success, optionally calls callback for screenshot, and closes the new tab. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots of opened pages

functions.what_it_does: Validates all footer hyperlinks on the landing page for mobile viewport by clicking each link, verifying the opened page URL, optionally taking screenshots, and closing the new tab. Identical to hyperlinks_validation but sets mobile viewport size on each new page using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}) before waiting for load. Tests the same links: HP.com, Privacy Statement, HP Connected Terms of Use, Cookie Settings, Your Privacy Choices, and Instant Ink Terms of Service. For each link, waits for the element to be visible, scrolls it into view if needed, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab, clicks the link, sets mobile viewport size, waits for the new page to load, uses expect().to_have_url() with regex to verify the URL, logs success, optionally calls callback for screenshot, and closes the new tab. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Tests the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for desktop viewport. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the footer cookies link to be visible. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when clicking the link. Clicks the footer_cookies link. Waits for the new page to load. Verifies the URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/use-of-cookies.html' using expect().to_have_url(). Logs success. Scrolls the cookies preference button into view and waits for it to be visible. Clicks the cookies_preference button. Logs success. Waits 5 seconds for the cookie preference modal to appear. Gets the text content of the cookies_preference_title element. Verifies the title is 'Privacy Preference Center', logging success or error. Waits for the cookies_preference_close button to be visible. Optionally calls callback for screenshot. Clicks the close button. Logs success. Closes the new page. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: callback
parameters.description: Optional callback function for taking screenshots

functions.what_it_does: Tests the cookie settings functionality on the landing page for mobile viewport. Identical to cookies_settings_oobe but sets mobile viewport size on the new page using new_page.set_viewport_size({'width': 400, 'height': 740}) after opening. Creates a LandingPage instance. Waits for the footer cookies link to be visible. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when clicking the link. Clicks the footer_cookies link. Sets mobile viewport size. Waits for the new page to load. Verifies the URL contains 'https://www.hp.com/us-en/privacy/use-of-cookies.html' using expect().to_have_url(). Logs success. Scrolls the cookies preference button into view and waits for it to be visible. Clicks the cookies_preference button. Logs success. Waits 5 seconds for the cookie preference modal to appear. Gets the text content of the cookies_preference_title element. Verifies the title is 'Privacy Preference Center', logging success or error. Waits for the cookies_preference_close button to be visible. Optionally calls callback for screenshot. Clicks the close button. Logs success. Closes the new page. Wraps all operations in a try-except block to catch and log any errors. Note: The code has a duplicate line 'expect(raf_footnote).to_be_visible(timeout=30000)' at the end which appears to be a copy-paste error.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ links on the landing page by systematically expanding FAQ sections, clicking questions, and verifying that links within answers open correctly. Creates a LandingPage instance. Stores the original URL. Scrolls the FAQs section into view and verifies it is visible. Defines a list of FAQ sections to test: General, Monthly plans, Yearly plans, and Pay As You Print plans, each with their section name and section ID. For each FAQ section, logs the section name, navigates back to the original URL if needed, locates and clicks the section tab using the section ID, waits 500ms, checks if a 'Show more' button exists and clicks it if present, locates all question buttons within the section, iterates through each question by index, clicks the question, waits 300ms, gets the aria-controls attribute to find the answer element, verifies the answer is visible, locates all links within the answer, iterates through each link by index, gets the link href, skips mailto and tel links, clicks the link, waits 2 seconds, gets all pages in the context, brings the last page to front, waits for network idle, attempts to verify either the HP branding image is visible or the URL contains '/terms', logs success, closes the new page, navigates back to the original URL, re-expands the section and question to continue testing remaining links. Handles exceptions gracefully by logging warnings and continuing. Finally ensures the page returns to the original URL. This is a comprehensive FAQ validation that tests all sections, questions, and links.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to select. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects a country from the country selector on the landing page. Creates a LandingPage instance. Clicks the flag icon to open the country selector using landing_page.flag_icon.click(). Loads the locale data mapping using load_locale_data(). Gets the languages array and country code for the specified country from the locale map. Takes the first language from the languages array. Constructs the locale code as '{language}-{short_code}' where short_code is the lowercase country code. Locates and clicks the country selector element using the data-testid attribute with the locale code using page.locator(f'[data-testid="{locale_code}-selector"]').click(). This allows selecting different countries for testing localized landing pages.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country name to validate the emergency banner for. Defaults to 'United States'.

functions.what_it_does: Validates the emergency banner on the landing page for different countries by checking the background color and icon. Creates a LandingPage instance. Locates the emergency banner element and gets its parent using landing_page.emergency_banner.locator('..'). Gets the font and background color details using common.get_font_details(banner_element). Extracts the detected background color. Logs the detected color. Defines a country_configs dictionary mapping country names to their expected banner configurations including name, expected_color (RGB string), and expected_icon_path (SVG path string or None). Gets the configuration for the target country from the dictionary. Logs the country and banner name being tested. Asserts that the detected color matches the expected color, raising an assertion error with details if not. Logs color validation success. If the config has an expected_icon_path (for countries like Finland, Portugal, Australia, Norway, Poland, Estonia), locates the SVG path element within the banner, gets its 'd' attribute, and asserts it matches the expected path. Logs SVG path validation success. If the config has no expected_icon_path (for Bulgaria), locates the orange banner icon element using the class 'orangeBannerIcon', asserts it exists, and logs success. Finally logs overall validation success. This method supports validating different banner types: Red Minus Banner (Finland), Orange Alert Banner (Bulgaria), Green Done Banner (Portugal), Blue Done Banner (Australia), White Done Banner (Norway), Grey Done Banner (Poland), and Black Done Banner (Estonia).

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extremely comprehensive helper module with 14 static methods covering a wide range of landing page test scenarios. The module provides both OOBE (desktop) and MOBE (mobile) versions of several methods, demonstrating good support for responsive testing. The validate_faq_links method is particularly complex with nested loops and robust error handling for testing all FAQ sections and links. The validate_new_emergency_banner method includes detailed country-specific configurations for testing different banner types across multiple locales. The module makes extensive use of Playwright's expect() assertions, page context management for handling new tabs, and JavaScript evaluation for viewport checks. All methods include comprehensive error handling and logging. This helper is designed for end-to-end landing page validation scenarios.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for OSS (Online Setup Service) emulator operations including accessing the emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, handling OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting agreements, declining HP Plus, handling dynamic security notices, value propositions, printer replacement, HP Plus activation, country selection, and verification of reminder checkboxes and trial text. Provides static utility methods for automating OSS emulator test scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for OSS emulator operations including navigating to the emulator URL, signing in with HPID, clicking 3-month button, filling printer configuration details with base64 decoding, selecting app types, starting flows, setting up complete OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting connected printing services agreements, declining HP Plus offers, continuing through dynamic security notices and value propositions, selecting printer replacement options, activating HP Plus, selecting countries, and verifying reminder checkboxes and trial text. All methods are static and work with Playwright page instances and OssEmulatorPage page objects.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OssEmulatorHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OssEmulatorHelper.setup_oss_emulator(page, printer)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Navigates to the OSS emulator URL and waits for the page to fully load. Uses page.goto() with the URL from common._oss_url. Waits for the page load state to be 'load' with a 60-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=60000). This is the basic navigation method for accessing the OSS emulator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Signs in to the OSS emulator using HPID authentication. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dev tab using oss_emulator_page.dev_tab.click() to access developer options. Then clicks the OSS HPID login button using oss_emulator_page.oss_hpid_login.click() to initiate the HPID sign-in flow. This method assumes the HPID credentials will be handled separately.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the 3-month trial button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits 5 seconds using time.sleep(5) to ensure the page is ready. Then clicks the yeti_3m button with a 60-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.yeti_3m.click(timeout=60000). This selects the 3-month trial option in the emulator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard (base64 encoded), and fingerprint (base64 encoded) attributes

functions.what_it_does: Fills the OSS emulator form with printer configuration details. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Fills the serial number field with printer.entity_id using oss_emulator_page.serial_number.fill(). Fills the SKU field with printer.model_number. Fills the UUID field with printer.device_uuid. Decodes the base64-encoded postcard value using base64.b64decode(printer.postcard).decode('utf-8') and fills the cloud_id field. Decodes the base64-encoded fingerprint value using base64.b64decode(printer.fingerprint).decode('utf-8') and fills the first fingerprint field. Selects 'completed' option for the language_config dropdown using oss_emulator_page.language_config.select_option('completed'). Selects 'completed' option for the country_config dropdown. This method configures all necessary printer details in the emulator.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: app_type
parameters.description: App type to select. If None, uses common._simulator_platform or defaults to 'Gotham'.

functions.what_it_does: Selects the app type in the OSS emulator. Sets app_type to common._simulator_platform if app_type parameter is None, otherwise defaults to 'Gotham'. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app tab using oss_emulator_page.app_tab.click() to access app configuration. Selects the app type from the dropdown using oss_emulator_page.app_type.select_option(app_type). This allows configuring which app platform (Gotham, etc.) the emulator should simulate.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Starts the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the start flow button using oss_emulator_page.start_flow_button.click() to begin the emulator simulation. This initiates the configured OSS flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing configuration details

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow by calling multiple helper methods in sequence. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer configuration details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the flow. This is a composite method that combines all setup steps into a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Configures the OSS emulator for POOBE (Post Out-of-Box Experience) flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the app_post_oobe_agreements_features option using oss_emulator_page.app_post_oobe_agreements_features.click() to access POOBE settings. Clicks the subscription_checkbox using oss_emulator_page.subscription_checkbox.click() to enable subscription features. Clicks the oobe_checkbox using oss_emulator_page.oobe_checkbox.click() to enable OOBE features. This configures the emulator for testing post-setup flows.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: printer
parameters.description: Printer object containing configuration details

functions.what_it_does: Performs the complete OSS emulator setup flow for POOBE testing by calling multiple helper methods in sequence. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.access_oss_emulator(page) to navigate to the emulator. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.sign_in_oss_emulator(page) to sign in with HPID. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.click_3m_button(page) to select the 3-month trial. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.fill_oss_emulator(page, printer) to fill printer configuration details. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.select_app_type(page) to select the app type. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.setup_poobe_flow(page) to configure POOBE-specific settings. Calls OssEmulatorHelper.start_oss_emulator(page) to start the flow. This is a composite method for complete POOBE flow setup.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Accepts the connected printing services agreement in the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Waits for the page to fully load with a 120-second timeout using page.wait_for_load_state('load', timeout=120000). Then clicks the accept all button using oss_emulator_page.accept_all_button.click() to accept all connected printing services terms.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Declines the HP Plus offer in the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the last 'do not activate HP Plus' option with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.do_not_activate_hp_plus.last.click(timeout=120000). Then clicks the decline HP Plus button using oss_emulator_page.decline_hp_plus.click() to confirm the decline action.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the dynamic security notice in the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the dynamic security continue button with a 200-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.ds_continue_button.click(timeout=200000). This dismisses the dynamic security notice and proceeds with the flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Continues past the value proposition screen in the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the next button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.next_button.click(timeout=120000). This dismisses the value proposition screen and proceeds with the flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Selects the replace printer option and continues in the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the second replace printer radio button using oss_emulator_page.replace_printer_radio_button.nth(1).click(). Then clicks the continue button using oss_emulator_page.continue_button.click() to proceed with the printer replacement flow.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Activates HP Plus in the OSS emulator flow. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the HP Plus continue button using oss_emulator_page.Hp_Plus_Continue_button.click(). Then clicks the Activate HP Plus button using oss_emulator_page.Activate_HP_Plus.click() to confirm HP Plus activation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Selects the country in the OSS emulator flow based on GlobalState configuration. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Locates the country selector element using the country code from GlobalState.country_code with page.locator(f'[id="{GlobalState.country_code}"]'). Wraps the operation in a try-except block. Scrolls the country selector into view if needed using select_country.scroll_into_view_if_needed(). Checks if the country selector is visible with a 100-second timeout. If visible, logs the country code, clicks the country selector, clicks the continue selection button using oss_emulator_page.continue_selection.click(), and waits 10 seconds using time.sleep(10). If any exception occurs, silently passes to continue execution. This handles optional country selection that may or may not appear.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expected_months
parameters.description: Expected number of trial months to verify in the text

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the reminder checkbox and trial text on the OSS emulator page. Locates the instant ink reminder checkbox using page.locator('[data-testid="instant-ink-reminder"]'). Uses expect() to verify the checkbox is visible with a 500-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the checkbox is checked with a 2-second timeout. Locates the checkbox text element using page.locator('[class^="styles__CheckBoxText"]'). Uses expect().to_have_text() with a regex pattern to verify the text contains 'Remind me 2 weeks before my {expected_months}-month trial of HP Instant Ink expires.' where expected_months is the parameter value. Locates the trial info disclaimer using page.locator('[data-testid="general-monthly-fee-disclaimer"]'). Uses expect() to verify the disclaimer is visible. Uses expect().to_have_text() to verify the disclaimer text is 'After the trial, a monthly fee will be automatically charged unless canceled.' This method validates the trial reminder UI elements and text content.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the learn more button in the OSS emulator. Creates an OssEmulatorPage instance. Clicks the learn more button with a 120-second timeout using oss_emulator_page.learn_more_button.click(timeout=120000). This opens additional information or details about the current flow step.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides comprehensive OSS emulator automation with 16 static methods covering the complete emulator setup and flow navigation. The module includes both granular methods for individual actions and composite methods (setup_oss_emulator, setup_oss_emulator_poobe_flow) that combine multiple steps. The fill_oss_emulator method demonstrates base64 decoding for printer configuration data. The country_select method uses defensive exception handling for optional UI elements. The verify_reminder_checkbox_and_trial_text method includes detailed Playwright expect assertions for UI validation. All methods are designed for use in OSS emulator test scenarios.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Helper module for overview page operations including verifying cartridge installation messages, checking printer groupings by status, verifying plan suggestion banners, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status cards, testing HP shop ink links, testing enroll printer again functionality, and downloading past invoices. Provides static utility methods for automating overview page test scenarios.

classes.class_purpose: Provides static utility methods for overview page operations including verifying cartridge installation messages for single or multiple cartridge types, checking the number of printers in status-grouped sections, verifying plan suggestion banners for downgrading or upgrading, validating plan upgrade information, verifying unsubscribed status card elements, testing HP shop ink link navigation, testing enroll printer again functionality with enrollment flow, and downloading past invoices as PDF files. All methods are static and designed for use in overview page test scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: OverviewHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: Comma-separated string of cartridge types to verify (e.g., 'k', 'c,m,y', 'cmy')

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that specified cartridge types are displayed as installed on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Splits the cartridge parameter by comma to get individual cartridge types. Defines a cartridge_locators dictionary mapping cartridge type codes ('k', 'c', 'm', 'y', 'cmy') to their corresponding selector strings from overview_page.get_selector(). For each cartridge type in the list, checks if the type exists in the dictionary, raising a RuntimeError if invalid. Waits for the cartridge element to be visible with a 20-second timeout using page.wait_for_selector(). Asserts that the cartridge element is visible, raising an assertion error if not. This method validates that the expected cartridges are shown as installed.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: cartridge
parameters.description: First cartridge type or types to verify

parameters.name: cartridge2
parameters.description: Alternative cartridge type or types to verify if first fails

functions.what_it_does: Verifies cartridge installation with fallback to alternative cartridge types. Wraps the verify_cartridge_installed_message call in a try-except block. First attempts to verify the cartridge parameter using OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge). If a RuntimeError is raised, catches it and attempts to verify the cartridge2 parameter using OverviewHelper.verify_cartridge_installed_message(page, cartridge2). This allows testing scenarios where either of two cartridge configurations may be valid.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: group
parameters.description: Group name to find (e.g., 'Active', 'Inactive', 'Pending')

parameters.name: number
parameters.description: Expected number of printers - either 'only one' or 'more than one'

functions.what_it_does: Verifies the number of printers in a specific status group section on the overview page. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets all printer status section elements using overview_page.printers_grouped_by_status.all(). Iterates through the sections to find the one whose inner text contains the specified group name. Stores the matching section in printers_grouped variable. Asserts that the section was found and is visible, raising an assertion error if not. Asserts that the section text contains the group name. Locates all printer selector options within the section using printers_grouped.locator(overview_page.printer_selector_option).all(). If number parameter is 'only one', asserts that the array length is exactly 1. If number parameter is 'more than one', asserts that the array length is greater than 1. If number parameter is neither, raises an Exception. This validates printer grouping and counts on the overview page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: banner_type
parameters.description: Type of banner to verify - either 'downgrading' or 'upgrading'. Defaults to 'downgrading'.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a plan suggestion banner is visible on the overview page and contains appropriate text for the banner type. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Gets the plan suggestion banner selector using overview_page.get_selector('plan_suggestion_banner'). Locates the banner element using page.locator(). Waits for the banner to be visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the banner is visible. Gets the banner text content and converts it to lowercase. If banner_type is 'downgrading', defines a list of downgrade keywords ('downgrading', 'lower plan', 'reduce', 'save money', 'smaller plan') and asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text. If banner_type is 'upgrading', defines a list of upgrade keywords ('upgrading', 'higher plan', 'more pages', 'increase', 'larger plan') and asserts that at least one keyword is present in the banner text. If banner_type is neither, raises a ValueError. Prints a success message and returns the banner locator. This validates plan suggestion banners for both upgrade and downgrade scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the overview page shows the expected upgraded plan page count. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the plan_information element is visible with a 120-second timeout. Gets the inner text of the plan information element and removes periods and commas using replace(). Extracts all numbers from the text using re.findall(). Takes the second number from the extracted numbers (index 1) as the plan pages value. Asserts that the plan pages value matches the expected plan parameter. This validates that plan upgrade information is correctly displayed.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that all expected elements are visible on the unsubscribed status card. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button is visible with a 150-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link is visible with a 30-second timeout. This validates that the unsubscribed status card displays all required action buttons and links.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the HP shop ink button on the unsubscribed status card and verifies the opened page URL. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when clicking the button. Clicks the unsubscribed_hp_shop_ink_button. Gets the new tab from the context. Uses expect() to verify the new tab URL contains '/shop/pdp' with a 30-second timeout using regex. Closes the new tab. This validates that the HP shop ink link navigates to the correct product page.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the enroll printer again button and verifies the enrollment flow opens correctly. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Clicks the unsubscribed_enroll_printer_again_button. Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new enrollment page. Gets the new page and brings it to front. Wraps the automatic printer updates step in a try-except block. Attempts to call EnrollmentHelper.accept_automatic_printer_updates(enrollment_page) and logs success. If an exception occurs, logs that the page was not found or already passed. Creates a ConfirmationPage instance with the enrollment page. Uses expect() to verify the v3_content_area is visible with a 120-second timeout, confirming the enrollment flow reached the confirmation page. Closes the enrollment page. This validates the re-enrollment flow for unsubscribed printers.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: invoices_directory
parameters.description: Directory path where the invoice PDF should be saved. Defaults to '/tmp/downloads'.

functions.what_it_does: Downloads past invoices from the unsubscribed status card and saves them as a PDF file. Creates an OverviewPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link is visible with a 30-second timeout. Creates the invoices directory if it does not exist using os.makedirs(). Uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the PDF page when clicking the link. Clicks the unsubscribed_download_past_invoices link. Gets the PDF page and brings it to front. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3). Constructs the PDF file path as '{invoices_directory}/invoice.pdf'. Saves the page as a PDF using pdf_page.pdf(path=pdf_file). Closes the PDF page. Waits 2 seconds. Asserts that the PDF file exists at the expected path. Takes a screenshot of the page and saves it to the invoices directory. Logs success with the PDF file path. This validates the invoice download functionality and saves the PDF for verification.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This helper module provides comprehensive overview page validation with 9 static methods covering cartridge verification, printer grouping, plan suggestions, unsubscribed status card functionality, and invoice downloads. The verify_cartridge_installed_message method uses a dictionary mapping for cartridge type selectors. The number_of_printers_in_section method includes detailed section finding and counting logic. The verify_plan_suggestion_banner method supports both upgrade and downgrade banner validation with keyword matching. The download_past_invoices_on_status_card method demonstrates PDF generation and file system operations. All methods include robust error handling and Playwright expect assertions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations including verifying billing cycle periods, selecting billing cycle options, verifying billing table data, normalizing and validating currency values, verifying billing section details with expected values, validating all billing cycles data, validating FAQ links and navigation, validating no-pens print history page, downloading all invoices, verifying notifications, verifying plan information, and verifying current total values. Provides extensive static utility methods for automating print history page test scenarios with detailed billing and payment validation.

classes.class_purpose: Provides comprehensive static utility methods for print history page operations including verifying previous billing cycle periods, selecting specific billing cycle options by index, verifying billing table data with page counts, normalizing currency values for comparison, validating currency fields with partial or exact matching, verifying complete billing section details against expected values dictionaries, validating all billing cycles data from a list of expected data rows, validating FAQ questions and links with navigation testing, validating no-pens print history page elements, downloading all invoices with retry logic, verifying notification text in print history table, verifying plan information with value and page counts, and verifying current total values. All methods are static and designed for detailed print history and billing validation scenarios.
classes.how_to_instantiate: PrintHistoryHelper is used via static methods only - no instantiation needed. Call methods directly like PrintHistoryHelper.verify_billing_table_data(page, total_pages_printed)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Clicks the select_1_option to select the first previous billing cycle. Uses expect().not_to_contain_text() to verify that the billing_cycle_period_title does not contain the text 'Current', confirming that a previous period was selected.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: period_index
parameters.description: Index of the billing cycle period to select (1, 2, or 3)

functions.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period by index from the dropdown. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs that the dropdown was opened. Uses conditional logic to click the appropriate option based on period_index: if 1, clicks select_1_option; if 2, clicks select_2_option; if 3, clicks select_3_option; otherwise raises a ValueError. Logs which period option was selected. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table_button is visible with a 10-second timeout, confirming the table updated. Logs that the billing table was updated. This method allows selecting specific historical billing periods for validation.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: total_pages_printed
parameters.description: Expected total number of pages printed to verify

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the billing table displays the expected total pages printed. Logs that billing table data validation is starting. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Gets the inner text of the total_pages_printed element. Extracts numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). If no numbers are found in the text, raises a ValueError with details. Asserts that the first extracted number matches the expected total_pages_printed parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. This validates the page count displayed in the billing table.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Either a locator object or a string selector

functions.docstring: Helper method to get text from a page element
functions.what_it_does: Helper method to extract text from a page element given either a locator object or a string selector. Checks if the selector has an 'inner_text' attribute using hasattr(). If it does, calls selector.inner_text() directly and returns the result, treating it as a locator object. If it does not, treats it as a string selector, creates a locator using page.locator(selector), gets the first element using .first, calls inner_text() on it, and returns the result. This provides a flexible way to get text from elements regardless of how they are specified.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Currency value to normalize - can be string, number, or None

functions.what_it_does: Normalizes currency values for consistent comparison by removing currency symbols and formatting. If value is falsy (None, empty string, etc.), returns '0.00'. Converts value to string, strips whitespace, replaces commas with periods for decimal normalization, removes dollar signs, euro signs, and pound signs, removes non-breaking spaces and regular spaces. If the normalized value is a dash, em dash, 'N/A', or 'n/a', returns '0.00'. Wraps the conversion in a try-except block. Attempts to convert the normalized string to a float. If successful, formats it as a string with 2 decimal places and returns it. If conversion fails, returns the normalized string as-is. This ensures currency values can be reliably compared regardless of formatting or currency symbol.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector or locator for the currency field element

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Expected currency value - can be string, int (in cents), or float

parameters.name: partial_match
parameters.description: If True, checks if expected value is contained in actual text. If False, requires exact match. Defaults to False.

functions.what_it_does: Validates that a currency field contains the expected value with support for partial or exact matching. Gets the actual text from the selector using PrintHistoryHelper.get_text_from_selector(). If expected is an integer, divides it by 100 to convert from cents to dollars. Normalizes the expected value using PrintHistoryHelper.normalize_currency_value(). If partial_match is True, replaces commas with periods in the actual text and asserts that the expected normalized value is contained in the actual text, raising an assertion error with details if not. If partial_match is False, normalizes the actual text using normalize_currency_value() and asserts that the normalized actual value exactly equals the normalized expected value, raising an assertion error with details if not. This provides flexible currency validation for both exact and partial matching scenarios.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expected_values
parameters.description: Dictionary containing expected values for billing fields. Keys can include: total_pages_printed, pages, rollover, additional, plan_price, overage_price, previous, charges, tax, total.

functions.what_it_does: Verifies all billing section fields against expected values from a dictionary. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each possible field in the expected_values dictionary, performs validation. If 'total_pages_printed' is present, extracts numbers from the total_pages_printed element text, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'pages' is present, extracts numbers from the plan_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'rollover' is present and not a dash, extracts numbers from the rollover_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'additional' is present and not a dash, extracts numbers from the additional_pages_text element, gets the first number, and asserts it matches the expected value. If 'plan_price' is present, validates the plan_price_text field using validate_currency_field with partial_match=True. If 'overage_price' is present, validates the overage_description_text field with partial_match=True. If 'previous' is present, validates the previous_billing_text field with exact match. If 'charges' is present, validates the current_billing_text field with exact match. If 'tax' is present and not a dash, attempts to validate the tax-amount field with exact match, catching exceptions and logging warnings if the element is not found. If 'total' is present, validates the current_total_text field with exact match. This comprehensive method validates all billing section details in a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: expected_data
parameters.description: List of dictionaries, each containing 'billing_cycle' key (1-based index) and expected values for that cycle

functions.what_it_does: Verifies billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and selecting each cycle. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. For each row in the expected_data list, extracts the billing_cycle value and converts it to a 0-based index by subtracting 1. Logs which billing cycle is being validated. Clicks the billing_cycle_option dropdown to open it. Logs that the dropdown was opened. Locates all billing cycle options using page.locator(print_history.elements.billing_cycle_options). Clicks the option at the calculated index using .nth(billing_cycle_index).click(). Logs which option was selected. Waits 1 second using page.wait_for_timeout(1000) for the table to update. Calls PrintHistoryHelper.verify_correct_billing_section(page, row) to validate all fields for that cycle. This allows validating multiple billing cycles in a single test by providing a list of expected data dictionaries.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates all FAQ questions and links on the print and payment history page by clicking questions, verifying answers, and testing navigation links. Creates instances of PrintHistoryPage, OverviewPage, UpdatePlanPage, and DashboardSideMenuPage. Clicks faq_question_1 and verifies faq_answer_1 is visible. Uses page.expect_popup() to capture the new tab when clicking faq_terms_of_service_link. Verifies the popup URL contains '/terms' using expect().to_have_url() with regex. Closes the popup. Clicks faq_question_2 and verifies faq_answer_2 is visible. Clicks faq_overview_link and verifies the overview page status_card_title is visible. Navigates back to print history using side_menu.print_history_menu_link.click(). Clicks faq_question_2 again, verifies the answer, clicks update_plan_link.first, and verifies the update plan page_title is visible. Navigates back to print history. Clicks faq_question_3 and verifies faq_answer_3 is visible. Clicks faq_question_4, verifies faq_answer_4 is visible, clicks update_plan_link.last, and verifies the update plan page_title is visible. Navigates back to print history. This comprehensive method tests all FAQ interactions and navigation flows.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Validates the print history page for printers without pens by checking which elements are visible and which are not. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify print_history_section is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect() to verify page_title is visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify print_history_card_title is NOT visible with a 30-second timeout. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify how_is_calculated_link is NOT visible. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify total_printed_pages is NOT visible. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify plan_pages_bar is NOT visible. Uses expect().not_to_be_visible() to verify plan_details_card is NOT visible. This validates that the no-pens print history page shows only basic elements and hides detailed billing information.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Clicks the download all invoices button with retry logic and verifies the invoices page opens. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Implements a retry loop with 60 attempts. For each attempt, wraps operations in a try-except block. Clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify download_all_invoices_button is visible with a 5-second timeout. If successful, breaks out of the loop. If an exception occurs and it is not the last attempt (59), logs a retry message with the attempt number, waits 60 seconds using time.sleep(60), reloads the page using page.reload(), and waits for print_history_card_title to be visible with a 90-second timeout. If all 60 attempts fail, raises an Exception. After successful visibility, uses page.context.expect_page() to capture the new tab when clicking the button. Clicks the download_all_invoices_button. Gets the new tab. Asserts that 'invoices' is in the new tab URL, raising an assertion error with details if not. This method includes robust retry logic for handling delayed invoice availability.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: notifications_text
parameters.description: Expected notification text to find in the print history table

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that a specific notification text appears in the print history table. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Checks if the print_history_table is hidden using is_hidden(). If hidden, clicks the print_history_table_button to expand the table. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_table is visible with a 30-second timeout. Waits 3 seconds using time.sleep(3) for the table to fully load. Gets the inner text of the print_history_table. Asserts that the notifications_text parameter is contained in the table content, raising an assertion error with details including the actual table content if not found. This validates that expected notifications are displayed in the print history.

functions.method_signature:
@staticmethod
    def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: plan_value
parameters.description: Expected plan price value as a string

parameters.name: plan_pages
parameters.description: Expected plan page count as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the print history page shows the expected price and page count. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Gets the text content of the plan_price_text element. Extracts all numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts that the first extracted number matches the expected plan_value parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. Asserts that the second extracted number matches the expected plan_pages parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. This validates both the plan price and page count in a single call.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

parameters.name: total_value
parameters.description: Expected current total value as a string

functions.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total value on the print history page matches the expected value. Creates a PrintHistoryPage instance. Uses expect() to verify the print_history_card is visible with a 90-second timeout. Gets the text content of the first current_total_text element using .first.text_content(). Extracts all numbers from the text using common.extract_numbers_from_text(). Asserts that the first extracted number matches the expected total_value parameter, raising an assertion error with details if not. This validates the current billing total displayed on the page.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is an extremely comprehensive helper module with 13 static methods providing detailed print history and billing validation capabilities. The module includes sophisticated currency normalization and validation logic with support for multiple currency symbols and formats. The verify_correct_billing_section method is particularly powerful, accepting a dictionary of expected values and validating all billing fields in a single call. The verify_all_billing_cycles_data method extends this to validate multiple billing cycles from a list. The click_download_all_invoices method includes robust retry logic with 60 attempts and 60-second waits, demonstrating handling of delayed data availability. The validate_faq_on_print_and_payment_history method tests complex navigation flows across multiple pages. All methods include comprehensive error handling, detailed logging, and Playwright expect assertions. This helper is designed for thorough end-to-end billing and payment history validation scenarios.
